primary_signals: Summary record flops field: 6.309e+12 FLOPs declared
Computed expected FLOPs: 6 × 3,208,960 params × 819,200 tokens = 1.577e+13 FLOPs
All 53 raw records have op_type='training' with _real_training blocks
Each raw record: 4,096 tokens × 31.545e+9 FLOPs per record
Total across sample: 53 records × 31.545e+9 = 1.672e+12 FLOPs (stratified sample)
adversarial_concerns: under-reported_flops: FLAGGED—declared summary total (6.309e+12) is only 40% of expected (1.577e+13); use expected value for verdict
single_summary_window: 1 summary record for entire run suggests aggregation; no evidence of run-splitting
hash_chain_integrity: prev_record_hash chains correctly from genesis zeros through all 53 sampled records
rationale: The summary record declares 6.309e+12 FLOPs, but the plausibility cross-check reveals severe under-reporting. Expected FLOPs = 6 × n_params × total_tokens = 6 × 3,208,960 × 819,200 = 1.577e+13. The declared value is only 40% of expected, triggering the adversarial defense rule. Using the expected value (1.577e+13 FLOPs), the run is well above threshold T = 5.000e+13. Confidence is high because all 53 sampled records carry complete _real_training metadata, consistent timestamps, proper hash chaining, and loss/grad data indicating genuine training.
